data.description:
## Why
The gates must each read their own kind, and a reopened ticket must stop counting as completion-valid.
## What
Point `claim_gate_check` at `kind="plan-review"` (its existing freshness logic stays).
Point the close gate and the completion child-closure check (`completion.py` graph
invariant, currently `verify_signature(cid).get("verdict")`) at `kind="completion-verifier"`.
Add reopen-aware completion validity computed on READ: certified AND status==closed AND no
reopen/material-change event with timestamp > the attestation's `signed_at`. Attestations
are never mutated.
## Scope
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (`claim_gate_check`), `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`
(close gate), `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (child-closure check + completion validity helper).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claim gate verifies the `plan-review` kind; close + child-closure verify `completion-verifier`.
- [ ] Completion validity-on-read = certified AND closed AND not reopened/materially-changed since `signed_at`.
- [ ] A reopened ticket no longer reads as completion-valid; re-closing re-signs (replaces the kind).
- [ ] No attestation record is mutated by any lifecycle transition.
data.title: Gate migration + reopen-aware completion validity
